stem: Evaluate \( \lim_{x \to \infty} \frac{[[a]]e^{x} + ([[b]])}{[[c]]e^{x} + ([[d]])} \); this value is the horizontal asymptote of the function as \( x \to \infty \).
solution: As \( x \to \infty \), \( e^{x} \to \infty \) dominates the constant terms, so \( \frac{[[a]]e^{x} + [[b]]}{[[c]]e^{x} + [[d]]} \to \frac{[[a]]e^{x}}{[[c]]e^{x}} = \frac{[[a]]}{[[c]]} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This inverts the ratio of the coefficients of \( e^{x} \).
This keeps only the numerator's coefficient.
This assumes the ratio approaches 1.
This is the limit of the constant terms, not the dominant exponential terms.
This multiplies the coefficients instead of dividing them.
This is off by one from the correct limit.

stem: Evaluate \( \displaystyle\lim_{x \to -\infty} \frac{[[a]]e^{x} + ([[b]])}{[[c]]e^{x} + ([[d]])} \); this value is the horizontal asymptote of the function as \( x \to -\infty \).
solution: As \( x \to -\infty \), \( e^{x} \to 0 \), so the exponential terms vanish and only the constant terms survive: \( \frac{[[a]]e^{x} + [[b]]}{[[c]]e^{x} + [[d]]} \to \frac{[[b]]}{[[d]]} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This uses the ratio of the exponential coefficients, which is correct only as \( x\to\infty \), not \( x\to-\infty \).
This reports only the numerator's constant term, not the ratio.
This assumes the ratio approaches 1.
This wrongly assumes the whole expression vanishes.
This multiplies the constant terms instead of dividing them.
This is off by one from the correct limit.

stem: A logistic growth model is given by \( f(x) = \dfrac{[[L]]}{1 + [[A]]e^{-[[k]]x}} \). Evaluate its carrying capacity \( \lim_{x \to \infty} f(x) \).
solution: As \( x \to \infty \), \( e^{-[[k]]x} \to 0 \) (since \( [[k]] > 0 \)), so the denominator \( \to 1 \). Hence \( f(x) \to \dfrac{[[L]]}{1} = [[answer]] \), the model's carrying capacity.
distractor_rationales: This wrongly assumes the whole expression decays to 0.
This divides by \( A \) as if the exponential term dominated instead of vanishing.
This uses the initial value at \( x=0 \) instead of the long-run limit.
This multiplies \( L \) and \( A \) instead of recognizing the denominator tends to 1.
This is off by one from the correct limit.
This is off by one from the correct limit.

stem: Let \( f(x) = [[a]]\arctan([[b]]x) + ([[c]]) \). Evaluate the horizontal asymptote value \( \lim_{x \to \infty} f(x) \).
distractor_rationales: This uses \( \pi \) instead of \( \pi/2 \) as the limit of \( \arctan \).
This forgets to add the constant shift \( c \).
This drops the \( \pi/2 \) factor entirely, treating \( \arctan \) as if it approached 1.
This reports only the vertical shift, ignoring the arctan term.
This is off by one from the correct limit.
This is off by one from the correct limit.

stem: Evaluate \( \displaystyle\lim_{x \to \infty} \left( [[d]] + \frac{[[a]]x + ([[b]])}{[[c]]e^{x}} \right) \); the result is the horizontal asymptote of this function.
solution: An exponential in the denominator grows faster than any polynomial in the numerator, so \( \dfrac{[[a]]x+[[b]]}{[[c]]e^{x}} \to 0 \) as \( x \to \infty \). Hence the whole expression \( \to [[d]] = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This assumes the fraction approaches the ratio of leading coefficients, as if numerator and denominator had the same growth rate.
This drops the constant shift \( d \) entirely.
This treats the fraction as if it approached the polynomial's own leading coefficient.
This adds all the constants together instead of recognizing the fraction vanishes.
This is off by one from the correct limit.
This is off by one from the correct limit.
